title: The One Where Monica Gets a New Roommate

lines.text: [Scene: Central Perk, Chandler, Joey, Phoebe, and Monica are there.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: There's nothing to tell! He's just some guy I work with!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: C'mon, you're going out with the guy! There's gotta be something wrong with him!
lines.korean: …분명 그 사람한테 뭔가 문제가 있을 거라고!
lines.explanation: gotta = (have) got to의 구어. 'there's gotta be ~'는 '분명/틀림없이 ~가 있을 거야'라는 강한 추측·확신.
examples: There's gotta be a better way to do this.
There's gotta be a reason he left.

english.text: All right Joey, be nice.
So does he have a hump? A hump and a hairpiece?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Wait, does he eat chalk?

lines.text: (They all stare, bemused.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Just, 'cause, I don't want her to go through what I went through with Carl- oh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Okay, everybody relax. This is not even a date. It's just two people going out to dinner and- not having sex.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Sounds like a date to me.

lines.text: [Time Lapse]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Alright, so I'm back in high school, I'm standing in the middle of the cafeteria, and I realize I am totally naked.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Oh, yeah. Had that dream.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Then I look down, and I realize there's a phone... there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Instead of...?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: That's right.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Never had that dream.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: No.

english.text: All of a sudden, the phone starts to ring.
Now I don't know what to do, everybody starts looking at me.
And they weren't looking at you before?!
Finally, I figure I'd better answer it,
and it turns out it's my mother, which is very-very weird, because- she never calls me!

lines.text: [Time Lapse, Ross has entered.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: (mortified) Hi.
lines.korean: (몹시 창피하고 풀이 죽은 채로) 안녕.
lines.explanation: mortified = 쥐구멍에 숨고 싶을 만큼 몹시 창피한/면목 없는. 이혼 직후라 풀 죽은 로스의 상태. (철자는 mortified)
examples: I was mortified when I forgot her name in front of everyone.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: This guy says hello, I wanna kill myself.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Are you okay, sweetie?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: I just feel like someone reached down my throat, grabbed my small intestine, pulled it out of my mouth and tied it around my neck...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Cookie?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (explaining to the others) Carol moved her stuff out today.

english.text: Ohh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (to Ross) Let me get you some coffee.

english.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Ooh! Oh! (She starts to pluck at the air just in front of Ross.)
lines.korean: (허공을 집어 뜯듯 잡아당기며)
lines.explanation: pluck = 손가락으로 집어 뽑다/잡아당기다(눈썹을 뽑다, 기타 줄을 튕기다 등). 여기선 피비가 로스의 '기운(aura)'을 정화한다며 허공을 집는 시늉. pluck at ~ = ~을 가볍게 잡아당기다.
examples: She plucked a grape from the bunch.
He plucked at the guitar strings.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: No, no don't! Stop cleansing my aura! No, just leave my aura alone, okay?

english.text: Fine! Be murky!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: I'll be fine, alright? Really, everyone. I hope she'll be very happy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: No you don't.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: No I don't, to hell with her, she left me!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: And you never knew she was a lesbian...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: No!! Okay?! Why does everyone keep fixating on that? She didn't know, how should I know?
lines.korean: …왜 다들 그것만 물고 늘어지는 거야?…
lines.explanation: fixate on ~ = ~에 (지나치게) 집착하다/계속 꽂혀 있다. 여기선 '왜 다들 그 얘기에만 매달리냐'는 짜증.
examples: Don't fixate on one bad review.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Sometimes I wish I was a lesbian... (They all stare at him.) Did I say that out loud?

english.text: I told mom and dad last night, they seemed to take it pretty well.
Oh really, so that hysterical phone call I got from a woman at sobbing 3:00 A.M., "I'll never have grandchildren, I'll never have grandchildren." was what? A wrong number?
Sorry.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Alright Ross, look. You're feeling a lot of pain right now. You're angry. You're hurting. Can I tell you what the answer is?

lines.text: (Ross gestures his consent.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Strip joint! C'mon, you're single! Have some hormones!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: I don't want to be single, okay? I just... I just- I just wanna be married again!

lines.text: (Rachel enters in a wet wedding dress and starts to search the room.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: And I just want a million dollars! (He extends his hand hopefully.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Rachel?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Oh God Monica hi! Thank God! I just went to your building and you weren't there and then this guy with a big hammer said you might be here and you are, you are!

lines.speaker: Waitress
lines.english: Can I get you some coffee?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (pointing at Rachel) De-caff. (to All) Okay, everybody, this is Rachel, another Lincoln High survivor. (to Rachel) This is everybody, this is Chandler, and Phoebe, and Joey, and- you remember my brother Ross?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Hi, sure!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Hi.

lines.text: (They go to hug but Ross's umbrella opens. He sits back down defeated again. A moment of silence follows as Rachel sits and the others expect her to explain.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: So you wanna tell us now, or are we waiting for four wet bridesmaids?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Oh God... well, it started about a half hour before the wedding. I was in the room where we were keeping all the presents, and I was looking at this gravy boat. This really gorgeous Limoges gravy boat. When all of a sudden- (to the waitress that brought her coffee)Sweet 'n' Lo?- I realized that I was more turned on by this gravy boat than by Barry! And then I got really freaked out, and that's when it hit me: how much Barry looks like Mr. Potato Head. Y'know, I mean, I always knew looked familiar, but... Anyway, I just had to get out of there, and I started wondering 'Why am I doing this, and who am I doing this for?'. (to Monica) So anyway I just didn't know where to go, and I know that you and I have kinda drifted apart, but you're the only person I knew who lived here in the city.
lines.korean: 결혼식 30분 전쯤, 선물 놔두는 방에서 그레이비 그릇을 보고 있었는데 갑자기 배리보다 그 그릇한테 더 끌린다는 걸 깨달았어. 겁이 확 나면서 '내가 왜, 누굴 위해 이걸 하지?' 싶더라고. 너랑 좀 멀어진 건 알지만, 이 도시에서 내가 아는 사람은 너뿐이라 왔어.
lines.explanation: gravy boat = 고기 요리에 끼얹는 그레이비 소스를 담아 따르는 길쭉한 배 모양 식기.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Who wasn't invited to the wedding.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Ooh, I was kinda hoping that wouldn't be an issue...

lines.text: [Scene: Monica's Apartment, everyone is there and watching a Spanish Soap on TV and are trying to figure out what is going on.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Now I'm guessing that he bought her the big pipe organ, and she's really not happy about it.

english.text: (imitating the characters) Tuna or egg salad? Decide!
(in a deep voice) I'll have whatever Christine is having.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: (on phone) Daddy, I just... I can't marry him! I'm sorry. I just don't love him. Well, it matters to me!

text.text: (The scene on TV has changed to show two women, one is holding her hair.)

english.text: If I let go of my hair, my head will fall off.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: (re TV) Ooh, she should not be wearing those pants.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: I say push her down the stairs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe, Ross, Chandler, and Joey
lines.english: Push her down the stairs! Push her down the stairs! Push her down the stairs!

lines.text: (She is pushed down the stairs and everyone cheers.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: C'mon Daddy, listen to me! It's like, it's like, all of my life, everyone has always told me, 'You're a shoe! You're a shoe, you're a shoe, you're a shoe!'. And today I just stopped and I said, 'What if I don't wanna be a shoe? What if I wanna be a- a purse, y'know? Or a- or a hat! No, I'm not saying I want you to buy me a hat, I'm saying I am a ha- It's a metaphor, Daddy!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: You can see where he'd have trouble.
lines.korean: (아버지가) 왜 이해를 못 하실지 알 만하네.
lines.explanation: 'You can see where he'd have trouble' = '그가 어디서 어려움을 겪을지(왜 이해 못 할지) 알 만하다'. he'd = he would이고, 이 would는 '~할 만하다/그럴 것이다'라는 추측. where는 '어느 지점에서/왜'에 가깝게 쓰임.
examples: I can see where this might get confusing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Look Daddy, it's my life. Well maybe I'll just stay here with Monica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Well, I guess we've established she's staying here with Monica...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Well, maybe that's my decision. Well, maybe I don't need your money. Wait!! Wait, I said maybe!!

lines.text: [Time Lapse, Rachel is breating into a paper bag.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Just breathe, breathe.. that's it. Just try to think of nice calm things...

lines.speaker: Phoebe

english.text: (sings) Raindrops on roses and whiskers and kittens, (Rachel and Monica turn to look at her.) bluebells and sleighbells and- something with mittens... La la la la... something and noodles with string. These are a few...

lines.korean: (좋은 생각을 떠올리려다 엉뚱한 노래를 엉터리로 흥얼거린다)
lines.explanation: 피비가 영화 '사운드 오브 뮤직'의 노래 'My Favorite Things'를 엉터리로 흥얼거리는 장면. bluebells = 종 모양의 보라색 야생화. sleighbells = 썰매에 매다는 방울. mittens = 손가락이 갈라지지 않은 벙어리장갑.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: I'm all better now.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: (grins and walks to the kitchen and says to Chandler and Joey.) I helped!
lines.korean: (씩 웃으며)
lines.explanation: grin = 이를 드러내고 활짝/씩 웃다. 만족·장난기·뿌듯함이 담긴 웃음. 여기선 피비가 자기가 도움 됐다며 뿌듯하게 씩 웃는 것.
examples: He grinned from ear to ear.

english.text: Okay, look, this is probably for the best, y'know? Independence. Taking control of your life.
The whole, 'hat' thing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: (comforting her) And hey, you need anything, you can always come to Joey. Me and Chandler live right across the hall. And he's away a lot.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Joey, stop hitting on her! It's her wedding day!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: What, like there's a rule or something?

lines.text: (The door buzzer sounds and Chandler gets it.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Please don't do that again, it's a horrible sound.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.english: (over the intercom) It's, uh, it's Paul.

english.text: Oh God, is it 6:30?
Buzz him in!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Who's Paul?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Paul the Wine Guy, Paul?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Maybe.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Wait. Your 'not a real date' tonight is with Paul the Wine Guy?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: He finally asked you out?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Yes!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Ooh, this is a Dear Diary moment.
lines.korean: 일기에 적어둘 만한 순간이네. (비꼼)
lines.explanation: 'Dear Diary'는 일기 첫머리에 쓰는 '일기장에게~'. 'a Dear Diary moment'는 '일기에 기록할 만큼 인상적인 순간'을 뜻하며, 여기선 챈들러가 살짝 비꼬듯 던지는 말.
examples: Aww, this is such a Dear Diary moment.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Rach, wait, I can cancel...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Please, no, go, I'll be fine!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (to Ross) Are, are you okay? I mean, do you want me to stay?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: (choked voice) That'd be good...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (horrified) Really?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: (normal voice) No, go on! It's Paul the Wine Guy!

english.text: What does that mean? Does he sell it, drink it, or just complain a lot? (Chandler doesn't know.)

lines.text: (There's a knock on the door and it's Paul.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Hi, come in! Paul, this is.. (They are all lined up next to the door.)... everybody, everybody, this is Paul.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Hey! Paul! Hi! The Wine Guy! Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: I'm sorry, I didn't catch your name. Paul, was it?

english.text: Okay, umm-umm, I'll just--I'll be right back, I just gotta go ah, go ah...
A wandering?
Change! Okay, sit down.
(Shows Paul in) Two seconds.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Ooh, I just pulled out four eyelashes. That can't be good.

english.text: Hey, Paul!
Yeah?
Here's a little tip, she really likes it when you rub her neck in the same spot over and over and over again until it starts to get a little red.
(yelling from the bedroom) Shut up, Joey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: So Rachel, what're you, uh... what're you up to tonight?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Well, I was kinda supposed to be headed for Aruba on my honeymoon, so nothing!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Right, you're not even getting your honeymoon, God.. No, no, although, Aruba, this time of year... talk about your- (thinks) -big lizards... Anyway, if you don't feel like being alone tonight, Joey and Chandler are coming over to help me put together my new furniture.
lines.korean: 그래, 넌 신혼여행도 못 가는구나, 참… 아니 근데 이맘때 아루바는… 그 왜, 큰 도마뱀들 말이야… 아무튼 오늘 밤 혼자 있기 싫으면, 조이랑 챈들러가 내 새 가구 조립 도와주러 오거든.
lines.explanation: 'Talk about ~!' = '~로 말하자면 정말 대단하다'는 강조 감탄. 로스가 아루바의 매력을 어색하게 띄우려다 엉뚱하게 '큰 도마뱀'으로 끝내는 머쓱한 농담. (여기 your는 특정 '너의'가 아니라 일반적 강조용)
examples: Talk about bad timing!
Talk about a comeback!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: (deadpan) Yes, and we're very excited about it.

english.text: Well actually thanks, but I think I'm just gonna hang out here tonight.
It's been a long day.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Okay, sure.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Hey Pheebs, you wanna help?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Oh, I wish I could, but I don't want to.

lines.text: Commercial Break

text.text: [Scene: The Subway, Phoebe is singing for change.]

english.text: (singing) Love is sweet as summer showers, love is a wondrous work of art, but your love oh your love, your love...is like a giant pigeon...crapping on my heart. La-la-la-la-la- (some guy gives her some change and to that guy) Thank you. (sings) La-la-la-la...ohhh!

lines.text: [Scene: Ross's Apartment, the guys are there assembling furniture.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: (squatting and reading the instructions) I'm supposed to attach a brackety thing to the side things, using a bunch of these little worm guys. I have no brackety thing, I see no worm guys whatsoever and- I cannot feel my legs.
lines.korean: 설명서엔 이 브래킷 같은 걸 옆판에 이 작은 벌레 같은 것들로 붙이라는데, 브래킷 같은 건 없고 벌레 같은 것도 하나도 안 보이고- 다리에 감각이 없어.
lines.explanation: 가구 조립 설명서를 못 알아본 로스가 부품을 유치하게 부르는 말 — brackety thing(브래킷 비슷한 것), worm guys(벌레처럼 생긴 작은 것들 = 나사·볼트류). whatsoever = 부정문 뒤에서 '전혀'를 강조(no ~ whatsoever = 전혀 ~ 없다).
examples: I have no idea whatsoever.
There's no doubt whatsoever.

text.text: (Joey and Chandler are finishing assembling the bookcase.)

english.text: I'm thinking we've got a bookcase here.
It's a beautiful thing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: (picking up a leftover part) What's this?

english.text: I would have to say that is an 'L'-shaped bracket.
Which goes where?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: I have no idea.

lines.text: (Joey checks that Ross is not looking and dumps it in a plant.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Done with the bookcase!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: All finished!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: (clutching a beer can and sniffing) This was Carol's favorite beer. She always drank it out of the can, I should have known.

english.text: Hey-hey-hey-hey, if you're gonna start with that stuff we're outta here.
Yes, please don't spoil all this fun.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Ross, let me ask you a question. She got the furniture, the stereo, the good TV- what did you get?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: You guys.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh, God.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: You got screwed.

english.text: Oh my God!

lines.text: [Scene: A Restaurant, Monica and Paul are eating.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.english: I know, I know, I'm such an idiot. I guess I should have caught on when she started going to the dentist four and five times a week. I mean, how clean can teeth get?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: My brother's going through that right now, he's such a mess. How did you get through it?
lines.korean: 걘 지금 완전 엉망이야 (정신을 못 차려)…
lines.explanation: be a mess = (감정·상태가) 엉망진창이다. 사람에 쓰면 '정신적으로 무너져 엉망인 상태'. 여기선 이혼으로 힘들어하는 오빠(로스)를 가리킴.
examples: After the breakup, she was a complete mess.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.english: Well, you might try accidentally breaking something valuable of hers, say her-

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: -leg?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.english: (laughing) That's one way of going through it! Me, I- I went for the watch.
lines.korean: (부술 물건으로) 시계를 노렸어.
lines.explanation: go for ~ = ~을 택하다/공략하다/노리다. 여기선 '망가뜨릴 대상으로 시계를 골랐다'는 뜻. ('go for it'=한번 해봐 등 다양하게 쓰임)
examples: I'll go for the steak.
You can do it — go for it!

lines.speaker: Monica

english.text: You actually broke her watch?
Wow! The worst thing I ever did was, I-I shredded by boyfriend's favorite bath towel.

lines.korean: 너 진짜로 걔 시계를 부쉈다고? 와! 내가 한 최악의 짓은 남자친구가 아끼던 목욕 수건을 갈가리 찢은 거야.
lines.explanation: shred = (종이·천 등을) 갈가리/잘게 찢다. 여기선 남자친구가 아끼던 수건을 갈기갈기 찢었다는 것. (명사 shredder = 문서 분쇄기. 대사의 'by'는 'my'의 오타)
examples: He shredded the documents before leaving.

english.text: Ooh, steer clear of you.
That's right.

lines.text: [Scene: Monica's Apartment, Rachel is talking on the phone and pacing.]

english.text: Barry, I'm sorry... I am so sorry... I know you probably think this is all about what I said the other night about you making love with your socks on, but it isn't... it isn't, it's about me, and I ju- (She stops talking and dials the phone.) Hi, machine cut me off again... anyway...

look, look, I know that some girl is going to be incredibly lucky to become Mrs. Barry Finkel, but it isn't me, it's not me. And not that I have any idea who me is right now, but you just have to give me a chance too... (The maching cuts her off again and she redials.)

lines.text: [Scene: Ross's Apartment; Ross is pacing while Joey and Chandler are working on some more furniture.]

english.text: I'm divorced! I'm only 26 and I'm divorced!
Shut up!
You must stop! (Chandler hits what he is working on with a hammer and it collapses.)
That only took me an hour.

Look, Ross, you gotta understand, between us we haven't had a relationship that has lasted longer than a Mento. You, however have had the love of a woman for four years. Four years of closeness and sharing at the end of which she ripped your heart out, and that is why we don't do it! I don't think that was my point!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: You know what the scariest part is? What if there's only one woman for everybody, y'know? I mean what if you get one woman- and that's it? Unfortunately in my case, there was only one woman- for her...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: What are you talking about? 'One woman'? That's like saying there's only one flavor of ice cream for you. Lemme tell you something, Ross. There's lots of flavors out there. There's Rocky Road, and Cookie Dough, and Bing! Cherry Vanilla. You could get 'em with Jimmies, or nuts, or whipped cream! This is the best thing that ever happened to you! You got married, you were, like, what, eight? Welcome back to the world! Grab a spoon!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: I honestly don't know if I'm hungry or horny.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Stay out of my freezer!

lines.text: [Scene: A Restaurant, Monica and Paul are still eating.]

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.english: Ever since she walked out on me, I, uh...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: What?..... What, you wanna spell it out with noodles?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.english: No, it's, it's more of a fifth date kinda revelation.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Oh, so there is gonna be a fifth date?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.english: Isn't there?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Yeah... yeah, I think there is. -What were you gonna say?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.english: Well, ever-ev-... ever since she left me, um, I haven't been able to, uh, perform. (Monica takes a sip of her drink.) ...Sexually.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (spitting out her drink in shock) Oh God, oh God, I am sorry... I am so sorry...

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.english: It's okay...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: I know being spit on is probably not what you need right now. Um... how long?
lines.korean: 지금 침 세례 받는 게 (네가) 필요한 건 아니겠지. 음… 얼마나 됐어?
lines.explanation: being spit on = (수동) 침/음료를 뱉음을 당하는 것 — spit on someone(~에게 침을 뱉다)의 수동형. being + 과거분사는 '~당하는 것'(수동 동명사). 모니카가 놀라 마시던 걸 폴에게 뿜은 걸 두고 '지금 침 세례 받는 게 필요한 건 아닐 텐데'라며 멋쩍게 농담하는 것. (spit 활용: spit-spit/spat)
examples: No one likes being lied to.
I hate being interrupted.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.english: Two years.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Wow! I'm-I'm-I'm glad you smashed her watch!

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.english: So you still think you, um... might want that fifth date?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (pause)...Yeah. Yeah, I do.

lines.text: [Scene: Monica's Apartment, Rachel is watching Joanne Loves Chaci.]

english.text: We are gathered here today to join Joanne Louise Cunningham and Charles, Chachi-Chachi-Chachi, Arcola in the bound of holy matrimony.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Oh...see... but Joanne loved Chachi! That's the difference!

lines.text: [Scene: Ross's Apartment, they're all sitting around and talking.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: (scornful) Grab a spoon. Do you know how long it's been since I've grabbed a spoon? Do the words 'Billy, don't be a hero' mean anything to you?
lines.korean: …'빌리, 영웅이 되지 마'라는 말, 뭔지 알아?
lines.explanation: 'Billy, Don't Be a Hero'는 1974년 히트곡으로, 전쟁에서 영웅이 되려다 죽는 군인 이야기. 로스는 '숟가락을 잡은(= 연애·결혼이라는 모험을 한)' 지 얼마나 됐냐며, 무모하게 뛰어들면 큰일 난다는 경고로 인용. 'Do the words ... mean anything to you?' = '~라는 말 뭔지 알아?/떠오르는 거 없어?'라며 교훈·암시를 상기시키는 표현.
examples: Do the words 'measure twice, cut once' mean anything to you?

english.text: Great story! But, I uh, I gotta go, I got a date with Andrea--Angela--Andrea... Oh man, (looks to Chandler)
Angela's the screamer, Andrea has cats.
Right. Thanks. It's June. I'm outta here. (Exits.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Y'know, here's the thing. Even if I could get it together enough to- to ask a woman out,... who am I gonna ask? (He gazes out of the window.)
lines.korean: 그게, 문제는 이거야. 설사 내가 여자한테 데이트 신청할 만큼 정신을 추스른다 쳐도… 누구한테 신청해?
lines.explanation: get it together = 정신을 차리다/마음을 다잡다(흐트러진 자신을 추스르다). 'get it together enough to ~' = ~할 만큼 (정신을) 추스르다. here's the thing = 문제는/요점은 이거야(본론을 꺼낼 때). ask someone out = ~에게 데이트 신청하다.
examples: Get it together, we have work to do.
You need to get your life together.

lines.text: [Cut to Rachel staring out of her window.]
[Scene: Monica's Apartment, Rachel is making coffee for Joey and Chandler.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Isn't this amazing? I mean, I have never made coffee before in my entire life.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: That is amazing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Congratulations.

english.text: Y'know, I figure if I can make coffee, there isn't anything I can't do.
If can invade Poland, there isn't anything I can't do.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Listen, while you're on a roll, if you feel like you gotta make like a Western omelet or something... (Joey and Chandler taste the coffee, grimace, and pour it into a plant pot.) Although actually I'm really not that hungry this morning.
lines.korean: 한창 잘나가는 김에, 혹시 웨스턴 오믈렛 같은 거라도 만들어야 할 것 같으면… (한 모금 맛보곤 인상 쓰며 화분에 부어버린다) 아, 근데 사실 나 별로 안 배고프다…
lines.explanation: be on a roll = 연달아 잘되는 중, 흐름을 탄 상태('while you're on a roll' = 한창 잘나가는 김에). 'if you feel like you gotta ~'의 if절은 권유를 완곡하게(여기선 비꼬듯) 돌려 말하는 장치 — '혹시 ~해야 할 것 같은 기분이 들면'. 직접 '오믈렛 만들어'라고 시키지 않고 그녀의 충동에 맡기는 척하는 농담이다. feel like (that) you gotta ~ = ~해야 할 것 같은 기분이 들다(gotta = got to). Western omelet = 햄·피망·양파를 넣은 오믈렛.
examples: If you feel like you have to say something, go ahead.

english.text: (entering, to herself) Oh good, Lenny and Squigy are here.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Morning. Good morning.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.english: (entering from Monica's room) Morning.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Morning, Paul.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Hello, Paul.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Hi, Paul, is it?

lines.text: (Monica and Paul walk to the door and talk in a low voice so the others can't hear. The others move Monica's table closer to the door so that they can.)

english.text: I had a really great time last night.
Thank you! Thank you so much!
Stop!
No, I'm telling you last night was like umm, all my birthdays, both graduations, plus the barn raising scene in Witness.
We'll talk later, okay?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.english: Yeah. (They kiss) Thank you. (Exits)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: That wasn't a real date?! What the hell do you do on a real date?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Shut up, and put my table back.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Okayyy! (They do so.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: All right, kids, I gotta get to work. If I don't input those numbers,... it doesn't make much of a difference...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: So, like, you guys all have jobs?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Yeah, we all have jobs. See, that's how we buy stuff.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Yeah, I'm an actor.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Wow! Would I have seen you in anything?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: I doubt it. Mostly regional work.
lines.korean: 글쎄, 아닐걸. 대부분 지역(소규모) 일이라.
lines.explanation: I doubt it = '글쎄, 아닐 거야'(부정적 추측). regional (work) = 전국구가 아닌 '지역 단위'의 연극·방송 일 — 무명 배우라는 머쓱한 뉘앙스.
examples: Will he show up? - I doubt it.

lines.speaker: Monica

english.text: Oh wait, wait, unless you happened to catch the Wee One's production of Pinocchio
, at the little theater in the park.

lines.korean: 아 잠깐, 잠깐, 혹시 공원 작은 극장에서 한 '꼬마들의 피노키오' 공연을 우연히 봤다면 모를까.
lines.explanation: wee = (구어, 특히 스코틀랜드·아일랜드) 아주 작은/꼬마. 'the Wee Ones' = 꼬마들/어린아이들 → 'the Wee One's production of Pinocchio' = 어린이(극단)의 피노키오 공연. 조이의 보잘것없는 연기 경력을 '공원 꼬마 극단 피노키오' 정도라며 비꼬는 것. happen to ~ = 우연히 ~하다, catch (a show) = (공연을) 보다.
examples: Did you happen to catch the game last night?

english.text: Look, it was a job all right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: 'Look, Gippetto, I'm a real live boy.'

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: I will not take this abuse. (Walks to the door and opens it to leave.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: You're right, I'm sorry. (Burst into song and dances out of the door.) "Once I was a wooden boy, a little wooden boy..."

english.text: You should both know, that he's a dead man. Oh, Chandler? (Starts after Chandler.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: So how you doing today? Did you sleep okay? Did you talk to Barry? I can't stop smiling.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: I can see that. You look like you slept with a hanger in your mouth.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: I know, he's just so, so... Do you remember you and Tony DeMarco?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Oh, yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Well, it's like that. With feelings.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Oh wow. Are you in trouble.
lines.korean: 야, 너 큰일 났다!
lines.explanation: 형태는 의문문(Are you in trouble)이지만 여기선 감탄으로 '너 진짜 큰일 났다!'는 뜻. 영어는 'Boy, are you in trouble!'처럼 주어-동사를 도치해 강한 감탄을 만듦. be in trouble = 곤경에 처하다.
examples: Oh, are you in for a surprise!

english.text: Big time!
Want a wedding dress? Hardly used.
I think we are getting a little ahead of selves here.
Okay. Okay. I am just going to get up, go to work and not think about him all day. Or else I'm just gonna get up and go to work.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Oh, look, wish me luck!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: What for?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: I'm gonna go get one of those (Thinks) job things.

lines.text: (Monica exits.)
[Scene: Iridium, Monica is working as Frannie enters.]

lines.speaker: Frannie
lines.english: Hey, Monica!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Hey Frannie, welcome back! How was Florida?

lines.speaker: Frannie
lines.english: You had sex, didn't you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: How do you do that?

english.text: Oh, I hate you, I'm pushing my Aunt Roz through Parrot Jungle and you're having sex!
So? Who?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: You know Paul?

lines.speaker: Frannie
lines.english: Paul the Wine Guy? Oh yeah, I know Paul.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: You mean you know Paul like I know Paul?

lines.speaker: Frannie
lines.english: Are you kidding? I take credit for Paul. Y'know before me, there was no snap in his turtle for two years.
lines.korean: …걔(폴)는 나 만나기 전 2년 동안 영 그쪽으로 활기가 없었어.
lines.explanation: 프래니가 만들어낸 우스꽝스러운 완곡 표현으로, 폴이 (프래니를 만나기 전) 2년간 성적으로 기력·활기가 없었다는 뜻. 직역하면 '거북이에 탁 무는 힘이 없었다' 정도로, 정형 표현이 아니라 캐릭터 특유의 능청스러운 농담.

lines.text: [Scene: Central Perk, everyone but Rachel is there.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: (sitting on the arm of the couch)Of course it was a line!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Why?! Why? Why, why would anybody do something like that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: I assume we're looking for an answer more sophisticated than 'to get you into bed'.

english.text: I hate men! I hate men!

lines.speaker: Phoebe

english.text: Oh no, don't hate, you don't want to put that out into the universe.

lines.korean: 그런 (부정적인) 기운을 우주에 내보내지 마.
lines.explanation: 뉴에이지·영적 사고에서 나온 표현 — 말·생각을 입 밖에 내면 그 기운이 '우주로 퍼져' 현실에 영향을 준다는 믿음. 피비답게 '미워하지 마, 그런 걸 우주에 내보내면 안 돼'라고 함.
examples: Stay positive and put good energy out into the universe.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Is it me? Is it like I have some sort of beacon that only dogs and men with severe emotional problems can hear?
lines.korean: 나 때문인가? 나한테 개랑 정서불안 남자들만 들을 수 있는 무슨 발신기라도 달렸나?
lines.explanation: beacon = 등대·봉화처럼 신호를 내보내는 불빛/표지. 모니카는 '나한테 개와 정서불안 남자들만 들을 수 있는 신호를 내는 발신기라도 달렸나?'라고 자조. (원래 시각 신호인데 'hear'와 엮어 과장한 농담)
examples: The lighthouse beacon guided the ships home.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: All right, c'mere, gimme your feet. (She starts massaging them.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: I just thought he was nice, y'know?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: (bursts out laughing again) I can't believe you didn't know it was a line!

lines.text: (Monica pushes him off of the sofa as Rachel enters with a shopping bag.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Guess what?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: You got a job?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Are you kidding? I'm trained for nothing! I was laughed out of twelve interviews today.
lines.korean: …면접마다 비웃음만 사고 (쫓겨나듯) 떨어졌어.
lines.explanation: laugh someone out of ~ = ~에서 비웃어 내쫓다. 수동태 'I was laughed out of the interviews' = '면접에서 비웃음만 받고 퇴짜 맞았다'는 과장된 자조.
examples: His idea got laughed out of the room.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: And yet you're surprisingly upbeat.
lines.korean: 그런데도 넌 의외로 기분이 좋아 보이네.
lines.explanation: (and) yet = '그런데도/그럼에도'(앞 내용과 대조). upbeat = 밝고 긍정적인, 기운찬. 면접에서 다 떨어졌는데도 신나 보이는 게 의외라는 챈들러의 비꼼.
examples: It was pouring rain, and yet she was cheerful.
He stayed upbeat despite the bad news.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: You would be too if you found John and David boots on sale, fifty percent off!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh, how well you know me...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: They're my new 'I don't need a job, I don't need my parents, I've got great boots' boots!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: How'd you pay for them?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Uh, credit card.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: And who pays for that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Um... my... father.

lines.text: [Scene: Monica and Rachel's, everyone is sitting around the kitchen table. Rachel's credit cards are spread out on the table along with a pair of scissors.]

english.text: Oh God, come on you guys, is this really necessary? I mean, I can stop charging anytime I want.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: C'mon, you can't live off your parents your whole life.
lines.korean: 평생 부모한테 얹혀살 순 없잖아.
lines.explanation: live off ~ = ~에 기대어/의존해 살다(남의 돈·지원으로 생활). 'live off your parents' = 부모 돈으로 얹혀살다.
examples: He still lives off his parents.
They live off the land.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: I know that. That's why I was getting married.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Give her a break, it's hard being on your own for the first time.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: You're welcome. I remember when I first came to this city. I was fourteen. My mom had just killed herself and my step-dad was back in prison, and I got here, and I didn't know anybody. And I ended up living with this albino guy who was, like, cleaning windshields outside port authority, and then he killed himself, and then I found aromatherapy. So believe me, I know exactly how you feel.
lines.korean: …포트 오소리티(버스터미널) 앞에서 (차) 앞유리를 닦던…
lines.explanation: windshield = 자동차 앞유리. Port Authority = 뉴욕 맨해튼의 대형 버스 터미널. 'cleaning windshields outside Port Authority'는 터미널 앞에 선 차의 앞유리를 닦아주고 푼돈을 받던, 빈민·노숙자의 거리 벌이를 가리킴 — 피비의 고단했던 과거.

lines.text: (Pause)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: The word you're looking for is 'Anyway'...

english.text: All right,
you ready?
No. No, no, I'm not ready! How can I be ready? "Hey, Rach! You ready to jump out the airplane without your parachute?" Come on, I can't do this!
You can, I know you can!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: I don't think so.

english.text: Come on, you made coffee! You can do anything! (Chandler slowly tries to hide the now dead plant from that morning when he and Joey poured their coffee into it.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: C'mon, cut. Cut, cut, cut,...

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Cut, cut, cut, cut, cut, cut, cut... (She cuts one of them and they cheer.)

english.text: Y'know what? I think we can just leave it at that. It's kinda like a symbolic gesture...
Rachel! That was a library card!

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Cut, cut, cut, cut, cut, cut, cut..

english.text: (as Rachel is cutting up her cards) Y'know, if you listen closely, you can hear a thousand retailers scream.

text.text: (She finishes cutting them up and they all cheer.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Welcome to the real world! It sucks. You're gonna love it!

lines.text: [Time Lapse, Rachel and Ross are watching a TV channel finishes it's broadcast day by playing the national anthem.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Well, that's it (To Ross) You gonna crash on the couch?
lines.korean: 소파에서 자고 갈래?
lines.explanation: crash = (지쳐) 쓰러져 자다; 'crash on the couch / at someone's place' = (남의 집) 소파에서 하룻밤 자다·신세 지다. 격식 없는 구어.
examples: Can I crash on your couch tonight?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: No. No, I gotta go home sometime.
lines.korean: 언젠가는 집에 가야지.
lines.explanation: sometime(한 단어) = (미래의) 언젠가/조만간. 구분: sometimes = 가끔, some time = 얼마간의 시간.
examples: Let's grab dinner sometime.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: You gonna be okay?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Hang on, look what I just found on the floor. (Monica smiles.) What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: That's Paul's watch. You just put it back where you found it. Oh boy. Alright. Goodnight, everybody.

english.text: Goodnight.

lines.text: (Monica stomps on Paul's watch and goes into her room.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Mmm. (They both reach for the last cookie) Oh, no-

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Sorry-

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: No no no, go-

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: No, have it, really, I don't want it-

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Split it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Okay. (They split it.) You know you probably didn't know this, but back in high school, I had a, um, major crush on you.
lines.korean: …(너한테) 완전 푹 빠져 있었어 (짝사랑).
lines.explanation: have a crush on ~ = ~에게 (보통 말 못 하고) 반하다/짝사랑하다. major crush = 엄청 크게 반한 상태. 주로 풋사랑·짝사랑에 씀.
examples: She had a crush on her classmate.
I had a major crush on him in college.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: I knew.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: You did! Oh.... I always figured you just thought I was Monica's geeky older brother.
lines.korean: …난 늘 네가 날 그냥 모니카의 범생이 오빠쯤으로 여긴다고 생각했어.
lines.explanation: figure (that) ~ = ~라고 생각하다/짐작하다(구어). think와 비슷하나 '나름 추측하다'는 뉘앙스. geeky = 공부벌레·괴짜 같고 좀 촌스러운(geek의 형용사형, 약간 자조·놀림조).
examples: I figured you'd be running late.
He's a bit geeky but really fun.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: I did.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Oh. Listen, do you think- and try not to let my intense vulnerability become any kind of a factor here- but do you think it would be okay if I asked you out? Sometime? Maybe?
lines.korean: 그… 있잖아, 내 극심한 감정적 취약함은 변수로 치지 말고- 너한테 데이트 신청해도 될까? 언젠가? 어쩌면?
lines.explanation: intense = (감정·정도가) 매우 강렬한/격한. 'my intense vulnerability' = 극도로 여리고 상처받기 쉬운 상태 — 이혼 직후라 마음이 몹시 약한 걸 (반쯤 농담으로) 강조하며 데이트를 신청.
examples: There was an intense silence in the room.
intense pain

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Yeah, maybe...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Okay... okay, maybe I will...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Goodnight.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Goodnight.

lines.text: (Rachel goes into her room and Monica enters the living room as Ross is leaving.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: See ya.... Waitwait, what's with you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: I just grabbed a spoon. (Ross exits and Monica has no idea what that means.)

lines.text: Closing Credits
[Scene: Central Perk, everyone is there.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: I can't believe what I'm hearing here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: (sings) I can't believe what I'm hearing here...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: What? I-I said you had a-

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: (sings) What I said you had...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (to Phoebe) Would you stop?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Oh, was I doing it again?

english.text: Yes!
I said that you had a nice butt, it's just not a great butt.
Oh, you wouldn't know a great butt if it came up and bit ya.
There's an image.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: (walks up with a pot of coffee) Would anybody like more coffee?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Did you make it, or are you just serving it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: I'm just serving it.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Yeah. Yeah, I'll have a cup of coffee.

english.text: Kids, new dream... I'm in Las Vegas.
(Rachel sits down to hear Chandler's dream.)
(To Rachel) Ahh, miss? More coffee?
Ugh. (To another customer that's leaving.) Excuse me, could you give this to that guy over there? (Hands him the coffee pot.) Go ahead. (He does so.) Thank you. (To the gang.) Sorry. Okay, Las Vegas.

lines.speaker: Chandler

english.text: Okay, so, I'm in Las Vegas...
I'm Liza Minelli-

lines.korean: 자, 그러니까, 나 라스베이거스에 있는데… 내가 라이자 미넬리인 거야-
lines.explanation: Liza Minnelli = 화려한 라스베이거스 쇼와 뮤지컬(예: 'Cabaret')로 유명한 미국 여가수·배우(주디 갈런드의 딸). 평범한 남자 챈들러가 '내가 그 화려한 여성 디바 라이자 미넬리가 된' 꿈을 꿨다고 하는 황당함이 웃음 포인트. 에피소드를 닫는 마지막 농담이자, 앞서 나온 챈들러의 이상한 꿈(벌거벗은 꿈) 시리즈와 이어지는 장면.

lines.text: End
